item.title: M#265 -Transparent White w/Matching Head-【4.64kg】 — $3,059.14
item.description:
$3,059.14 · Brand New · GUITAR PLANET

Aelier Z is highly regarded for its outstanding playability, the reliable quality typical of Japanese-made basses, and, above all, its one-of-a-kind sound character. This particular instrument features a beautiful black-and-white contrast created by the combination of a translucent “Trans White” finish that reveals the wood grain and an ebony fingerboard.

Based on the manufacturer’s flagship model, the 5-string “Magnitude” (M#265), this instrument features an ash body paired with an ebony fingerboard. It is characterized by a combination of the manufacturer’s original pickups and a Bartolini preamp. Furthermore, by incorporating the manufacturer’s proprietary “Spectrum Boost” technique into the preamp wiring, it creates Atelier Z’s unique character—a sound that is exceptionally crisp and cuts through the mix with ease.

In terms of playability, the scale length is 34 inches—the same as a standard 4-string bass. With a nut width of 47.5 mm and a fingerboard radius of 400R (approx. 15.7"), these specifications are standard for a 5-string bass. However, the neck—molded to a moderately thin profile and featuring a satin-finished back—combines with these specs to deliver exceptional playability.

When considering the playing style inferred from its signature sound character, a low-action setup is recommended. The exceptional precision of the neck and fingerboard finish—which can withstand this setup—combines seamlessly with the unique sound characteristics mentioned earlier and the outstanding playability, resulting in a model that exudes a distinctive personality and presence that stands out among the many manufacturers on the market.

This bass excels in both sound and appearance.

We encourage you to take this opportunity to consider it.


SPEC
Body: Ash
Neck: Maple
Neck Scale: 34"
Fingerboard: Ebony,400R
Number of Fret: 21
Pickups: Atelier Z JBZ-5
Preamp: Bartolini XTCT w/Spectrum Boost
Control: Vol, Balancer, Tone (Active/Passive), Treble/ Bass
Case: Original Gig Bag
Weight: 4.64kg
Serial Number: 042367

---------------------------------------
Thank you for checking GUITAR PLANET.

-Make sure to log on to PAYPAL for ordering.
-All items are tracked and insured.

Items may sold at storefront before changing status.
Buyer is responsible for import fee if applied.

item.title: 2025 Mayones Cali 4 Fretless Mini Bass Eye Poplar - Trans Pink Matt | Polish Custom Boutique Travel Bass Short Scale Active/Passive Electronics Neck Through Headphone Preamp 2.01kg | CoA Case — $2,150.53
item.description:
$2,150.53 · Mint · Simon's Gear Garage

I am offering here a Mayones Cali 4 Fretless Mini Bass from 2025, a custom 4-string mini fretless bass handcrafted at the Mayones fine guitar and bass workshop in Gdańsk, Poland, featuring a resonant swamp ash body (with a total weight of just 2kg!), 11-ply maple-mahogany-wenge-amazakoe neck in neck-through construction with 44mm nut width, premium ebony fingerboard with 24 fret markers and 16’’ radius, Mayones Velvetrone Noble 4 humbucker pickup, short 436mm scale length, high-quality Mayones X24 two-piece bass bridge, Mayones MB-1070 tuners, and built-in headphone amplifier (!) in a gorgeous Trans Pink (top) / natural matte finish with black hardware in mint condition, including gray Mayones Hybrid case and professional setup with 30-85 Mayones Cali strings.



Under the Mayones label, the custom guitar and bass workshop in Gdańsk has been building—since 1982 under the leadership of the founding Dziewulska family—not only highly sought-after guitars in the heavy genre but also superbly crafted basses for a wide range of styles, from heavy to progressive, jazz, and fusion. No wonder, then, that renowned bassists such as Eugene Abdukhanov (Jinjer), Maurizio Rolli, and Federico Malaman have chosen a Mayones bass. Mayones instruments combine the best in hardware and woods with a bass-building approach that pays meticulous attention to detail, resulting in instruments that perform at the very highest level visually, sonically, and in terms of playability.


With the Cali Mini Bass, Mayones is pursuing a concept that is as unusual as it is fascinating. Despite its extremely compact dimensions, it is a full-fledged instrument designed specifically for maximum mobility, creative practice, travel, or modern studio applications. The ultra-short scale length of just 436mm makes the Cali one of the most extraordinary basses on the market and opens up entirely new playing possibilities.


The 2025 fretless model featured here has a resonant swamp ash body, which—despite its low total weight of just 2.01 kg—delivers an astonishingly mature tonal foundation. The three-dimensionally grained “AAA” Eye Poplar top gives the instrument a distinctive look and harmonizes perfectly with the eye-catching Trans Pink finish. The meticulously crafted 11-ply neck-through construction, made of maple, mahogany, wenge, and amazakoe, ensures maximum stability and remarkably direct vibration response.


Particularly interesting is the fretless version with its high-quality ebony fingerboard and 24 frets. This produces an extremely expressive tone with smooth transitions, a vocal character, and outstanding dynamics. From singing solo lines to warm jazz sounds and modern fusion and ambient tones, this instrument offers an exceptionally broad range of expression.


When plugged into an amp, the Mayones Velvetrone Noble 4 humbucker delivers an astonishingly powerful and defined basic tone, which is complemented by the state-of-the-art Musashi Nitōryū 3EQ electronics with an integrated headphone amplifier and Aux-In. This means the bass can not only be used in the traditional way with amplifiers or recording setups, but is also ideal as a portable practice and travel instrument. The extensive tone control options allow for precise adjustment to a wide variety of musical requirements.


A sound sample of a similar model can be found above at the end of the photo gallery.



Technical Specifications:

  • Origin: Gdańsk, Poland
  • Year of Manufacture: 2025
  • Model: 4-string mini bass
  • Body: Solid swamp ash
  • Top: “AAA” Eye Poplar
  • Neck: 11-ply maple-mahogany-wenge-amazakoe neck, neck-through
  • Fingerboard: Ebony with fretlines and dot fret markers
  • Fingerboard radius: 16''
  • Frets: 24
  • Nut width: 44 mm
  • Headstock: Matching headstock
  • Pickup: 1x Mayones Velvetrone Noble 4 Humbucker
  • Electronics: 1x Vol. (push/pull for active/passive mode), 1x Middle, 1x Treble/Bass, Musashi Nitōryū 3EQ – dual-output preamp & headphone amp incl. Aux-In
  • Bridge: Mayones X24 two-piece bass bridge
  • Tuners: Mayones MB-1070
  • Hardware: Black
  • Finish: Trans Pink (top), Natural (body and neck)
  • Scale length: 436mm (17.1")
  • Weight: 2.01kg
  • Serial No.: Cali 659
  • Includes gray Mayones Hybrid Case, manufacturer’s paperwork, and accessories



This Mayones Cali 4 Fretless Mini Bass is a one-of-a-kind custom instrument and is only available as a special order.


This bass is in mint condition and in perfect working order. Visually, it shows practically no visible wear (see photos). The frets are like new.


The instrument has been professionally set up with 30-85 Mayones Cali strings, and I (a long-time guitar teacher) will ship it fully insured with DHL for €29.99 within Germany or for €69.99 within the EU. Worldwide shipping is available—individual shipping costs upon request.


All images shown are of the actual instrument for sale.

The short-scale design provides a punchy and distinct tonal character that has made it a favorite among bass players for decades. Equipped with a set of powerful humbucker pickups, it delivers a robust and versatile sound capable of handling both gritty rock grooves and smoother, soulful lines.

The SG Standard Bass Faded is designed to meet the needs of modern musicians while paying homage to its storied past.

Grassroots guitars is a brand developed by the increasingly reputable ESP of Japan- priced for the entry level market but boasting high attention to precision.

This version of ESP's traditional-style Jazz Bass features a beautiful see-through finish and white-bound neck with block inlays.

Its hard maple neck and Japanese "tamo" ash body with string through bridge pack a solid punch that transfers well through original pickups with plenty of tone variation featurig a series mode (tone knob pull) for a powerful humbucker sound.

・The punchy sound characteristic of a ash body
・A through-bridge design that enhances harmonics and sustain
・An angled neck joint that provides easy access
・Humbucker mode for a wide range of sound shaping

High cost performance makes this a great bass for beginners and gigging bassists alike.

item.title: MVT#715 Olympic White Made in Japan 【4.69kg】 — $1,539.27
item.description:
$1,539.27 · Good · GUITAR PLANET

The body is crafted from carefully selected alder. The bolt-on construction combines a maple neck with a rosewood fingerboard, achieving both a vintage-like harmonic structure and a responsive attack. You can experience the natural resonance of an instrument that’s been played for years right from the moment you pick it up.


By deliberately adopting a 21-fret configuration, this model expands the range of musical expression without compromising its vintage character. It handles position shifts and phrasing—which often posed limitations on traditional vintage models—with ease, adapting seamlessly to modern playing styles.

Additionally, a lacquer finish is used on the neck, a key factor in playability. Its smooth texture and comfortable feel help maintain high concentration even during long playing sessions, reliably supporting the player’s expression.


The guitar is equipped with the “MVT-5P,” an Atelier Z original passive pickup designed exclusively for this model. Although passive, it delivers an extremely responsive attack, organically harmonizing rich, full-bodied lows, dense mids, and lustrous, refined highs.

It delivers a tone with depth and richness when played fingerstyle, a clear attack and sense of speed when played slap, and a well-defined, commanding sound when played with a pick, presenting a highly polished sonic image regardless of genre or playing style.

The rear pickup features a ’70s-style routing, offering the wild, aggressive projection and distinctive attack—with a peak in the high-mid range—characteristic of 1970s JB models.


The neck is in excellent condition; it remains straight and shows no signs of twisting, warping, or buckling. The truss rod has ample adjustment range and functions well, ensuring the guitar is ready for long-term use.

The tuning pegs show some overall dulling. The body and neck have minimal damage, consisting only of minor dents and scuffs, with no critical scratches.


Comes with a Gator hard case.

SPEC
Body:Alder
Neck:Maple
Neck Scale:34"
Fingerboard:Rosewood
Number of Fret:21
Pickups: ATELIER Z MVT-5P
Control:2Volume,Tone
Weight:4.69kg
Case : Hard Case

We've received a used Xotic 5-string JB model, known for its excellent craftsmanship and sound quality!

This popular model features an ash body and maple fingerboard, offering the feel of a vintage Jazz Bass combined with the versatility of an active design. It boasts a high-quality Trilogic 2 preamp, providing a wide range of sounds, including a 3-band equalizer, a mini-switch for Mid control (Off/On - @400Hz/800Hz - ±12dB), a Bright switch (Off/On - @6KHz/12K - ±18dB), and push/pull functionality on the front volume knob for Active/Passive switching, and push/pull functionality on the rear volume knob for Single/Ham connection switching, allowing for genre-defying sound shaping.

The pickguard has been replaced from the original to a burl wood type approximately 6mm thick, allowing for smoother playing without fingers getting too caught when slapping.

There are scratches, dents, and paint cracks on the headstock and body, but it is still perfectly playable.
The truss rod has plenty of adjustment range, and the neck is in good condition.

Weight ≈ 4.38 kg
Accessories: Original gig bag

A used ESP "Viper Bass" has arrived!

Featuring a high-quality mahogany body and a rigid 3-piece maple neck, this guitar produces a powerful, fat sound with a thick low end. While it incorporates a CINNAMON active circuit, the cut function has been removed for simplicity, and the wiring has been optimized to increase the boost level, resulting in an extreme specification.
*Mid control and gain can be adjusted using the trimmers located within the control cavity.

The pickups have been replaced from Seymour Duncan to EMG, resulting in a more powerful sound. The volume knob has also been replaced as a result of this change, and the active/passive mode cannot be switched.

There are some minor scratches, light dents, and tarnishing on the parts, but it is in relatively good condition.
The truss rod has plenty of adjustment range, and the neck is in good condition. The nut on the 1st string is a little low, causing slight buzzing when played open, but there is no buzzing when fretted, making it perfectly playable.

Weight ≈ 3.70 kg
Accessories: ATELIERZ soft case

-----------------------------------

Used EDWARDS E-AMAZE-AS now in stock!

While the basic material configuration—an ash body with a hard maple neck and fretboard—is the same as the ESP AMAZE-AS, the use of different parts has resulted in a more versatile bass.
Lightweight HIPSHOT Ultralite tuners are used to reduce neck dive. The bridge is a GOTOH 404SJ-5 with steel saddles. The strings can also be strung from the back of the body according to preference. The pickups are CL-P-J5-2 pickups made by ESP Custom Lab and developed specifically for the E-AMAZE. In addition to thorough shielding using the Custom Lab's unique copper foil, they employ a split coil that provides hum-canceling effect, achieving low noise even as J-style pickups.
The EQ circuit incorporates a specially modified IC from the ESP HALIBUT, tuned specifically for the E-AMAZE. The volume pot doubles as a switch pot; in its normal state, it operates in active mode using the EQ circuit, but pulling the knob up switches it to passive mode, canceling the EQ circuit. Additionally, a separate master tone control is provided, which works in both active and passive modes. This is also a switch pot; in its normal state, it uses a 0.023μF capacitor, but pulling the knob up switches it to a 0.047μF capacitor.

By intentionally omitting a primer coat, the wood grain (pores) remains visible, allowing you to appreciate the natural texture of the wood with a thin coating. Furthermore, the use of lacquer for the topcoat gives it a distinctive feel and sheen.

There are minor scratches, light dents, and stand marks, but it is in good working order and can be played without any problems.
The truss rod has plenty of adjustment range, and the neck is in good condition.

Weight ≈ 4.75 kg
Accessories: Original gig bag

item.title: Atelier Z M#265 CTM -Matte Black/MH- #012376[4.49kg][Made in Japan][IKE011] — $2,580.54
item.description:
$2,580.54 · Brand New · Kurosawa Music Ikebukuro

-----

*Caution!!

To prevent fraudulent purchases, we do not accept PayPal guest checkout.

Please create your PayPal account before make the payment.

-----

Welcome to

Kurosawa Ikebukuro! We can ship anywhere all over the world!!

Thank you for looking! Feel free to ask for more details. Also, please carefully read our

shop policy below the guitar description!

For Paypal payments, please register your address in your Paypal account in advance.

------------------------------------------

【Ikebukuro Store Custom Order】

A special edition exuding a commanding, jet-black presence.


We are pleased to announce the arrival of the M#265 CTM, featuring a striking matte black finish and a matching headstock.


The M#265 CTM is highly acclaimed as a flagship 5-string model from ATELIER Z. While retaining the exceptional playability and overwhelming sound quality for which the series is known, this particular instrument adopts a matte black finish—subduing the gloss to create a more stoic, sophisticated aesthetic.


The first thing that catches the eye is its bold, all-black appearance.


Unlike standard gloss black, the matte black finish used here minimizes light reflection, resulting in a look that balances composure with a sense of gravitas. Eschewing flashy ornamentation in favor of a style that captivates through the instrument's inherent presence, this is truly a bass designed for the mature player.


It also features a matching headstock finished in the same color as the body. This unified design, flowing seamlessly from headstock to body, creates a taut, streamlined impression.


Rather than competing through ostentation, it commands attention with its overwhelming aura.


Its look—radiating a unique presence on stage—is sure to stir the desire of many players to make it their own.


In terms of sound, it delivers the outstanding response and exceptional tonal range characteristic of ATELIER Z.


The combination of an ash body, maple neck, and maple fingerboard produces a sound with lightning-fast attack, featuring clearly defined lows and cutting, articulate highs.


The low-B string—crucial for a 5-string model—is remarkably clear; it produces a massive low-end without ever losing definition. Excellent string separation ensures that even complex phrases and chord work are reproduced with every single note ringing out clearly.


The onboard ATELIER Z original JBZ-5 pickups faithfully reproduce the nuances of the player's touch. When played fingerstyle, it produces a powerful, solid low end and a rich midrange; when slapped, it delivers the sharp attack and shimmering highs characteristic of ATELIER Z. It offers exceptional versatility across genres, including rock, pop, fusion, funk, and R&B.


It is also equipped with a Bartolini XTCT preamp and Spectrum Boost.


This setup allows for extensive sound shaping while preserving the natural character of the instrument's raw tone; by adjusting the treble and bass, you can freely control the sound, ranging from a modern hi-fi tone to a punchy, low-end-focused sound.


It supports active/passive switching, offering flexibility for use in various environments, such as live performances and studio recording sessions.


In terms of playability, it boasts the high level of craftsmanship expected of ATELIER Z.


The 34-inch scale provides natural string tension and excellent neck balance, ensuring a comfortable playing experience despite being a 5-string model. It facilitates smooth fingering and stress-free position shifts, allowing you to maintain high performance even during long playing sessions.


The hardware features reliable GOTOH GB528 tuners and the ATELIER Z original BB518 bridge. These components ensure excellent tuning stability and rich sustain, offering the high reliability required for professional use.


It offers overwhelming authority rather than mere flashiness.


The substantial feel of the matte black finish, the cohesive look of the matching headstock, and the superb sound and playability that are hallmarks of ATELIER Z.


The M#265 CTM is a must-try instrument for anyone seeking a bass that exudes a quiet yet commanding presence on stage.

item.title: Atelier Z M#265 CTM -Transparent Blue/MH- #012288[Made in Japan][IKE011] — $2,580.54
item.description:
$2,580.54 · Brand New · Kurosawa Music Ikebukuro

Thank you for looking! Feel free to ask for more details. Also, please carefully read our shop policy below the guitar description!

------------------------------------------------------------

[Ikebukuro Store Custom Order]

We have received the M#265 CTM, a bass with a commanding presence created by the interplay of a vibrant blue finish and the beautiful grain of its ash body.


The M#265 CTM is a flagship 5-string model from ATELIER Z, highly acclaimed by bassists everywhere. This particular instrument features a stunning "Transparent Blue" finish and a matching headstock; it combines exceptional playability and sound with a visual appeal that brings true pride of ownership.


The first thing that catches the eye is the beautifully executed Transparent Blue finish. Colored in a vivid blue that allows the dynamic grain of the ash wood to show through, the finish reveals different nuances depending on the viewing angle and lighting.


The color scheme—shifting from deep blue to a brilliant, luminous blue—looks spectacular under stage lighting. While it offers a touch of glamour distinct from simple black or natural finishes, it remains tasteful rather than flashy, exuding an air of luxury and sophistication.


It also features a matching headstock that coordinates with the body color. This creates a unified look from head to body, evoking the special feel of a custom-made instrument. It is truly a special bass that satisfies the desire for ownership the moment you open the case.


In terms of sound, you can fully enjoy the overwhelming response and wide frequency range characteristic of ATELIER Z instruments.


The sound produced by the classic combination of an ash body, maple neck, and maple fingerboard is defined by a fast-attacking response and a clear, defined tone. It delivers a balanced output across the entire spectrum, with distinct separation between frequency ranges, ensuring a solid presence within any musical ensemble.


The reproduction of the low B-string—crucial for a 5-string model—is outstanding; it produces a massive low-end sound while maintaining clear articulation and definition. It is ready for immediate use across a wide range of genres, including modern pop, rock, fusion, gospel, and R&B.


The onboard ATELIER Z original JBZ-5 pickups faithfully reproduce the nuances of the player's touch. When played fingerstyle, it delivers a powerful, solid low end and rich mid-range; when slapped, it produces the snappy attack and shimmering highs characteristic of ATELIER Z instruments.


Equipped with a Bartolini XTCT preamp and Spectrum Boost, it offers extensive sound-shaping capabilities. Effective control of the treble and bass allows for seamless adjustment—ranging from a high-fidelity, cutting tone to a commanding sound that leverages a massive low end.


It also features an active/passive switching function, ensuring flexibility across various environments, from live performances to studio recording. Its versatility across genres is a key part of its appeal.


The playability reflects the high level of craftsmanship expected from ATELIER Z. The 34-inch scale provides natural string tension and a stable neck feel, ensuring a comfortable playing experience despite being a 5-string model. Smooth position shifts and excellent handling accommodate everything from technical playing to groove-oriented styles.


Hardware includes GOTOH GB528 tuners and the ATELIER Z original BB518 bridge. These components ensure superior tuning stability and rich sustain, offering the high reliability required for professional use.


It features a beautiful Transparent Blue finish, a matching headstock that commands attention, and the outstanding sound and playability unique to ATELIER Z.


The M#265 CTM is a must-try for players seeking an instrument that stands out on stage, as well as for those who refuse to compromise on either sound or aesthetics.


【Specifications】


Scale: 34"


Neck: Maple 1P


Fingerboard: Maple 21F


Body: Ash


Tuning Keys: GOTOH GB528


Bridge: ATELIER Z BB518


Pickups: ATELIER Z JBZ-5


Preamp: Bartolini XTCT + Spectrum Boost


Controls: Master Volume, Balancer, Tone (Active ON/OFF), Treble/Bass


Weight: 4.43kg

item.title: 【outlet】OOPEGG / Supreme Collection Stormbreaker Bass Prototype FR 4.165kg #21058【GIB Hyogo】【Plek-adjusted】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $2,871
item.description:
$2,871 · Excellent · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

This product has been leveled using PLEK.

The string height at the 12th fret is 1.5mm for the G string and 2.0mm for the E string (2.3mm for the Low-B string).

OOPEGG is a high-end guitar brand born in Japan in 2019.

Since its establishment, the brand has achieved global success both domestically and internationally.

Due to extremely limited production numbers, it is not widely available.

However, it is highly regarded by many world-class professional musicians, and its popularity continues to grow.

In fact, it is used by renowned musicians such as Bruno Mars, his bassist Jamareo Artis, and Shawn Stockman (Boyz II Men). (Titles omitted)

The Supreme Collection, described as "neo-bizarre," is a semi-custom order series that boasts original designs that are not bound by existing formats such as Fender or Gibson.

This model is a Stormbreaker Bass.

The body is made of alder, and the fingerboard is made of rosewood.

The neck is a 5-piece laminated neck of maple and mahogany.

This technique of laminating multiple pieces of wood together reduces left-right and individual differences, resulting in greater stability.

The scale length is 31 inches.

Fender Jazz Basses have a 34-inch scale length. Gibson EB-3s and Fender Mustang Basses use scale lengths of around 30 inches.

While it uses a relatively short scale length, it still exhibits a loose, loud low-end nuance similar to basses with shorter scale lengths.

However, the playability doesn't feel difficult due to this.

The headstock angle and bridge bend angle were apparently researched extensively, resulting in a finish that feels natural to those accustomed to playing 34-inch basses.

The bridge is an original design and manufactured in Greece.

By being custom-made and making fine adjustments to the woodworking, it provides the player with the appropriate tension.

The tuning machines are HIPSHOT Ultralite lollipop.

There's a reason for using lightweight tuners; this setup provides excellent weight balance.

The frets are medium jumbo size, and Luminlay frets are mounted on the sides. While the design is retro, the use of modern technology makes it truly neo-bizarre.

The neck pickup is a Guild BS-1 Bisonic, and the bridge pickup is a Serek LZN-B90 Single Coil.

Combining pickups from different brands is not common among bass brands, and could be considered unique to OOPEGG.

Even so, the excellent balance achieved in the mix is ​​a testament to the designer's skill.

The controls include neck pickup volume/tone, bridge pickup volume/tone, and a 4-way rotary switch.

The switch options are neck, neck + bridge, neck + bridge (passive filtered), and bridge.

The neck pickup has a deep tone, while the bridge pickup is crisp and powerful.

Even with the mix of settings, the balance is excellent, and with the filter applied, the low-frequency quantity is skillfully controlled.

In some studios or ensembles, the low frequencies might feel too prominent, but this filter will be useful in those situations.

A unique and exceptional item, rarely released by brands that purely focus on bass production.

We want many people to experience this.

GIB's craftsmen will adjust it for easy playability before delivery.

Accessories: Gig bag, warranty, manual

Text: Ryoma Terao

Inspector: Hayakawa

Product photos: Tei

This is an official manufacturer outlet item.

There may be some cosmetic scratches.

A warranty is provided for everything except cosmetic issues.

(Warranty is only valid for purchases made within Japan)

●Price includes VAT & Duties & Shipping Fee
Free Shipping Worldwide. All import duties and taxes are included in the price. There are no hidden fees upon delivery.

If you return a product, you will be responsible for all shipping and customs fees.

Note: All electronic devices, such as effect pedals and amplifiers, are set for use in Japan.
Please be sure to check the voltage and power specifications of your country before purchasing and using.

1960's Avalon "The Shaggs" AV-1B 4-String Bass Guitar - Tiger Maple Sunburst - Made In Japan

Step back into the golden age of Japanese guitar craftsmanship with this vintage 1960's Avalon "The Shaggs" AV-1B 4-String Bass Guitar. Featuring a striking Tiger Maple Sunburst finish, this rare and eye-catching bass embodies the unique styling and character that made many Japanese-built instruments from the era highly collectible today.

The AV-1B delivers classic vintage tone and feel, with a comfortable neck, distinctive body design, and retro appointments that stand out on stage or in any collection. Whether you're a collector of vintage Japanese instruments, a fan of garage rock aesthetics, or a player seeking something truly unique, this Avalon bass offers plenty of charm and personality.

item.title: 【new】Sire / P6-5 SGM 3.645kg #2N25090077【GIB Hyogo】【Plek-adjusted】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $1,410
item.description:
$1,410 · Excellent · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

This product has been leveled using PLEK.

The string height at the 12th fret is 1.5mm for the G string and 2.0mm for the E string (2.3mm for the Low-B string).

Sire became famous as the brand that released basses supervised by Marcus Miller.

The quality of the guitars and basses they manufacture is extremely high, and they are highly regarded not only by Marcus Miller fans but also by many others.

This is the new P6-5 model.

While their usual models are classic in style, this one is more modern.

The body top is made of ash, and the back is made of mahogany.

This likely means they've used high-quality Indonesian mahogany while ensuring both good looks and sound.

The fretboard and fret finish are outstanding.

It's finished to a high standard, not only at this price point but also comparable to higher-priced models.

The pickup layout is a PB-type split-coil plus a JB-type single-coil.

This is the so-called PJ style. There is no control plate; the controls are mounted directly to the body top.

These are named the Marcus Super-PJ Revolution Set Pickup set and Marcus Heritage-3 (No-Mid Frequency).

The controls consist of volume, tone, balance, treble, middle, and bass (push-pull active/passive switching).

While models like the P7 used a middle frequency, this model has simpler controls.

In that respect, it could be considered a more classic active bass.

Furthermore, by fixing the middle frequency range, the manufacturer has designed the overall sound with a more intentional purpose.

This is something that only Sire, with its accumulated knowledge from numerous products, can achieve.

The sound is well-balanced from low to high frequencies, and is characterized by a crisp tone.

Even those who regularly play JB basses should find it satisfying.

This is an outlet item.

Due to long-term storage, there may be some scratches on the exterior.

A warranty is provided for everything except the exterior.

The K.Yairi YB-13E is one of the rarest Japanese-made acoustic basses.
Featuring a coral rosewood body, a deep-toned coral rosewood top and back, and a mahogany neck paired with a rosewood fingerboard, this instrument naturally produces the airy quality characteristic of acoustic basses along with a rich, full-bodied mid-to-low range.

The body offers the lightness typical of a hollow-body design while still producing a resonant, full-bodied tone. When played through a line output, the FISHMAN Prefix Pro preamp refines the tonefs definition, resulting in a well-balanced sound thatfs easy to work with in real-world bass-playing situations.

When played acoustically, the woody, soft attack isnft overly prominent, resulting in a tone that blends well in acoustic ensembles or home recording environments. When played through a line output, it offers a stable dynamic range and the flexibility to adjust its character depending on the situation.

The Bacchus 5-string bass features a beautiful burl poplar top and a practical active circuit.

The Bacchus WL5-BP-AC/M inherits the design philosophy of the high-end WOODLINE series while offering exceptional value for the price.

Furthermore, the use of a brass saddle and brass neck plate ensures rich sustain and a stable tone.

This 5-string bass offers an excellent balance of looks, playability, and sound, making it versatile enough to excel across a wide range of genres.

item.title: Atelier Z Beta5 [037727] (06/23) — $2,043.74
item.description:
$2,043.74 · Good · ISHIBASHI MUSIC

The BETA5 combines the high-fidelity sound characteristic of Atelier Z with the exceptional playability of a 24-fret design.

Building on the powerful, clear sound inherited from the M Series, this 5-string model from the BETA Series features a 24-fret design and a deep cutaway, allowing for stress-free playing all the way up to the high frets.

Its ash body, maple neck, and maple fingerboard deliver a crisp attack and clear bass tones. Equipped with a preamp featuring an active/passive switch and a mid-range control, it supports a wide range of sounds, from slap to fingerstyle.

Based on the serial number on the back of the headstock and the control layout, this instrument is believed to have been manufactured relatively early in the series. It features specifications different from more recent models, and its ability to evoke the history of Atelier Z is part of its appeal.

This model fully embodies the exceptional craftsmanship unique to high-end Japanese-made basses, combining a clear, well-defined sound?even on the low strings?with excellent playability.
Since the tuning peg for the 1st string was found to be defective, only that peg has been replaced with a new one.
There is a chip in the clear finish on the fingerboard binding on both sides of the 19th fret.
Overall, there are some scratches, dents, and tarnish on the metal parts, but there is no excessively noticeable damage.
There are no noticeable issues with the neck, and it is in good playing condition.

item.description:
$543.27 · Good · ISHIBASHI MUSIC

The FSR model offers the nimble playability and ease of handling unique to a short-scale guitar.

Based on the Vintage Modified series?inspired by 1960s Mustang Basses?this model is a limited-edition Fiesta Red version released specifically for the Japanese market. Its 30-inch short scale and compact body offer exceptional playability, making it popular with a wide range of players, from guitarists switching to bass to serious bassists.

The body is crafted from basswood, while the neck and fingerboard are made of maple. Equipped with Duncan Designed split-coil pickups, it delivers the rich, warm low end characteristic of short-scale instruments, along with a clear, punchy sound. Its intuitive operation via simple volume and tone controls is another key feature.

Additionally, the through-body 4-saddle bridge ensures sufficient string tension. It balances ease of handling with a stable playing feel, making it versatile for a wide range of situations, from practicing at home to live performances.

item.title: Atelier Z M#245 CTM -Transparent Green-#042377[4.31kg][Made in Japan][IKE011] — $2,457.66
item.description:
$2,457.66 · Brand New · Kurosawa Music Ikebukuro

Thank you for looking! Feel free to ask for more details. Also, please carefully read our shop policy below the guitar description!

------------------------------------------------------------

[Ikebukuro Store Custom Order Model]


We are pleased to announce the arrival of a special-specification M#245 CTM, featuring a "Transparent Green" finish that exudes a presence that is both vibrant and elegant.


The M#245 CTM is highly acclaimed as one of ATELIER Z's classic, popular models. While retaining the perfected sound and playability of the original, this specific instrument—crafted through our store's own custom order—features a rare Transparent Green finish and a matching headstock, making it a truly unique piece.


The first thing that catches the eye is, without a doubt, its stunning appearance. The "see-through" green finish—relatively rare among standard domestic models—fully showcases the powerful, beautiful grain of the ash wood while creating an impression that balances freshness with a sense of luxury.


Its translucent hue sets it apart from ordinary green finishes; the color shifts in character depending on the lighting and viewing angle, displaying a range of tones from deep emerald green to vibrant green. It radiates a special presence that cannot be found in black or natural finishes.


Furthermore, the matching headstock—color-coordinated with the body—creates a beautiful, unified design from head to body. This unique touch, characteristic of a custom-ordered model, delivers a sense of pride and joy the moment you open the case.


In terms of sound, the instrument fully embodies the signature ATELIER Z character.


Built with classic specifications—an ash body, maple neck, and maple fretboard—it delivers a sound defined by sharp, responsive attack and an exceptional frequency range. The tight, powerful low end, clear and cutting mid-to-high range, and the exhilarating attack during slap playing represent the true essence of the ATELIER Z sound.


The onboard ATELIER Z original JBZ-4 pickups faithfully reproduce the player's touch and nuances. They output a thick, solid low end and a commanding midrange for fingerstyle playing, while delivering a snappy attack and sparkling highs for slap playing—ensuring a strong, distinct presence within any musical ensemble.


It is also equipped with a Bartolini XTCT preamp and "Spectrum Boost," allowing for a wide range of sound shaping possibilities. Effective control of the treble and bass allows you to freely sculpt your tone, ranging from clear, high-fidelity sounds to rich, powerful, and weighty tones.


It features an active/passive switching function, offering the flexibility to adapt to various situations, from recording sessions to live performances. It possesses the high potential to excel across a wide range of genres, including funk, fusion, pop, and rock.


In terms of playability, it boasts the high level of craftsmanship synonymous with ATELIER Z. The 34-inch scale provides natural string tension and balanced neck stability, ensuring a stress-free, comfortable playing experience. Fingering and position shifts are smooth, allowing the player to maintain high performance levels during long gigs or recording sessions.


The hardware includes reliable GOTOH GB528 tuners and the ATELIER Z original BB419 bridge. These components deliver excellent tuning stability and rich sustain, fully supporting the player's expressive capabilities.


This is a custom-ordered model from our shop, featuring the signature outstanding sound quality and playability of ATELIER Z, combined with a "Transparent Green" finish rarely seen on the market.


It is the classic M#245 model, yet it offers something unique for those who want an instrument that stands out from the crowd.


This special instrument is designed to strongly appeal to the player's desire for ownership.


It commands a striking presence on stage, showcases a beautiful ash grain, and delivers the exceptional sound that only ATELIER Z can provide.


We invite you to consider this instrument—one that offers complete satisfaction in looks, sound, and playability.


【Specifications】


Scale: 34"


Neck: Maple 1P


Fingerboard: Maple 20F


Body: Ash


Tuning Keys: GOTOH GB528


Bridge: ATELIER Z BB419


Pickups: ATELIER Z JBZ-4


Preamp: Bartolini XTCT + Spectrum Boost


Controls: Master Volume, Balancer, Tone (Active ON/OFF), Treble/Bass


Weight: Approx. 4.31kg

Accessories: Gig bag,
----------------------------------

Please contact us if you have any questions.
*Notice: As this product is being sold in our retail store and website, there is a possibility that it may be sold out when you place your order. We will cancel the sale if this happens.

Please read our Policies Below:
Customs Tax
International Buyers Please Note:
Import duties, taxes, and charges are not included in the item price or shipping cost. These charges and any import procedures are the buyer's responsibility. Please check with your country's customs office for this info prior to bidding or buying. Customs fees are normally charged by the shipping company or collected when you pick the item up. These fees are not additional shipping charges. We won't under-value merchandise or mark the item as a gift on customs forms. Doing that is against U.S. and international laws.

Shipping

Please Note:

We only ship to PayPal registered address.Please contact us if you reside in countries in which we do not specify shipping service and cost. Please be aware that delivery could be delayed depending on customs inspection, in case of natural disasters, and other reasons such as postal strikes.


Payment

To prevent fraudulent purchases, we do not accept PayPal guest checkout.

Please create your PayPal account before make the payment.

A used BASS VI, now converted into a full-fledged baritone guitar, has arrived!
With maintainability in mind, the nut has been replaced to accommodate 13-63 gauge strings. The standard tuning is B-E-A-D-G-B from the bass string side, but you can play in standard tuning by placing a capo on the 5th fret. It is set so that, with a capo on the 5th fret and the string at the 12th fret, the bass string measures 2.0 mm and the treble string measures 1.5 mm.
The body shows signs of wear, including scuffs, dents with paint chips, and cracks and chips in the finish around the neck pocket, as well as traces of filled screw holes. item.title: Fender MIJ HERITAGE 60s Jazz Bass FL 3CS [JD20014358] (06/23) — $1,894.99
item.description:
$1,894.99 · Good · ISHIBASHI MUSIC

A special MIJ Heritage 60s Jazz Bass, featuring a fretless design and epoxy coating?crafted exclusively for a select few players.

Modelled after Jacofs signature coating, the epoxy layer is approximately 1.5 mm thick. This thickness creates a unique gresonanceh between the neck and strings, resulting in a slightly slower attack than a standard thin coating. It possesses a charm reminiscent of a human voice.

It bears the marks of having been used on countless stages, with several scratches and dents visible. The dots on the neck (which are stickers and can be removed) were applied by the previous owner to help keep track of the fret positions even when the stage lights go out.

An aftermarket gig case is included.

item.title: Atelier Z M#265 CTM -Transparent Black- #042363[Made in Japan][IKE011] — $2,580.54
item.description:
$2,580.54 · Brand New · Kurosawa Music Ikebukuro

Thank you for looking! Feel free to ask for more details. Also, please carefully read our shop policy below the guitar description!

-----------------------------------

We are proud to present this custom-ordered model featuring a stunning "Transparent Black" finish!

This particular instrument boasts exceptional wood grain—a textbook example of what makes an ash body so desirable—with a wild, expressive pattern that pairs perfectly with the chic, see-through black finish to create a truly striking look. It also features a matching headstock for a unified, cohesive aesthetic.

Regarding the color: the translucent black blends with the natural hue of the wood, resulting in a shade that leans slightly toward brown rather than a flat, pale black; this gives the instrument a sophisticated yet rich, character-filled appearance.


ATELIER Z is a brand favored by top players around the world. This high-end 5-string bass embodies the brand's true essence—delivering tight low-end, outstanding response, and incredible sonic clarity—making it a powerful, ready-to-use tool for both live performances and studio recording.

The beautiful ash body showcases a "Transparent Black" finish that highlights the natural grain. The bold, distinctive ash grain emerging from the deep black hue creates a premium feel, while the matching headstock ensures a unified look. Simple yet commanding in presence, it also offers excellent visibility on stage.

Ash is used for the body—the heart of the sound—while maple is employed for the neck and fingerboard. This combination achieves the fast attack and wide frequency range characteristic of ATELIER Z, balancing the powerful low-end impact required of a 5-string bass with exceptional tonal clarity. The low B-string rings out powerfully without losing definition, ensuring a commanding presence that cuts through the mix in any ensemble.

The onboard ATELIER Z original JBZ-5 pickups faithfully reproduce every nuance of the player's touch. They deliver a thick, solid tone for fingerstyle playing and an explosive attack with sparkling highs for slapping, allowing you to fully enjoy the exhilarating sound character that defines ATELIER Z. The combination of the Bartolini XTCT preamp and Spectrum Boost allows for a wide range of sound shaping while preserving the instrument's natural, organic tone. With effective control over treble and bass frequencies, the bass adapts flexibly to various styles—from the high-fidelity sound required for funk and fusion to the powerful tones suited for rock and pop. It also features an active/passive switching function, making it easy to select the right sound for any situation.


ATELIER Z’s signature playability is fully evident here as well. The neck profile fits the hand perfectly, and the smooth fingerboard action allows for effortless execution of everything from technical phrases to groove-oriented playing. The 34-inch scale provides a comfortable, natural string tension, ensuring excellent playability despite being a 5-string model.


For hardware, the instrument utilizes reliable GOTOH GB528 tuners and the ATELIER Z original BB518 bridge. These components ensure superior tuning stability and rich sustain, giving you confidence in demanding environments, whether on stage or in the studio.


The M#265 CTM combines the crisp attack, expansive tonal range, and beautiful aesthetics that define ATELIER Z. It is a model we confidently recommend not only to those seeking a professional-grade 5-string bass ready for immediate use, but also to players looking to elevate their sound and playing experience to the next level.


Scale: 34"
Neck: Maple1P
Fingerboard: Maple 21F
Body: Ash
Tuningkey: GOTOH GB528
Bridge: ATELIER Z BB518
Pickups :ATELIER Z JBZ-5
Preamp: Bartolini XTCT+Spectrum boost
Controls: Master Volume, Balancer, Tone(Active ON/OFF), Treble/Bass


Case：Gig Bag
Weight：4.40kg
----------------------------------

item.title: Hofner 500/1 shortscale violin bass von 1964 in Sunburst (Restauriert, Vintage, Hföern, Beatle) — $3,586.21
item.description:
$3,586.21 · Very Good · PhilThy Guitars

Up for sale is this beautiful 1964 Höfner 500/1 Violin Bass with the famous Höfner Franz Pix 511B Staple "Nova Sonic" pickups, the same ones that this famous musician has on his bass, which is why so many musicians want one. The 1964 500/1 differs from the 1963 model only in the binding and 3D logo, which I think makes the 500/1 basses look more premium. This particular 500/1 has undergone a complete restoration by me and is in excellent overall condition. It has a beautiful used look and a truly lovely tiger maple top on the back. In short, a dream 1964 500/1 bass that, thanks to a new neck reset, also plays wonderfully with a low action.

The 500/1 is from 1965! It shows signs of use! There are blemishes, dings and dongs, scratches and cracks on the headstock, body and parts. The pickups are rewound by me. Pickups I have rewound are authentic. I use the same type of wire; the same tape and crepe material; the same direction of winding; same number of turns; Same assembly technique – there is no audible difference to an original. All pickups work, as well as every switch and potentiometer. The bass has get a neck reset by me with hide glue, eliminating a common weak point in these basses. The fretboard has been dressed and neck and string action have been setuped. The 500/1 is ready to use immediately in the studio, at home, or on stage.

I gave the violin bass a comprehensive overhaul. I thoroughly cleaned, disinfected and polished the paintwork. Mechanics were disassembled, cleaned, polished and re-lubricated as well as every single screw. All electrical system treated, cleaned and checked for grounding. Every single component is disassembled, checked, cleaned and polished. The neck is adjusted for good string action, the fretboard is cleaned, dressed, oiled and sanded. No refret required. The 500/1 is octave clean and setuped.

! The Bass is ready to roll !

More pictures are available and can be shown upon request.

  • 30″ / 76cm Shortscale
  • pot dates: 250K 204 (CW20 1964)
  • 2x famous Höfner Franz Pix „Nova Sonic“ staple pickups (Humbucker)
  • Maple body with laminated spruce top
  • 3 piece maple neck (new neck reset with hide glue)
  • Rosewood fretboard with binding, 22 frets
  • Perloid dot inlays
  • small control panel: 2x Volume , Rhythmn/Solo Switch, Bass On & Treble On Switch
  • Vintage 60s Höfner Nickel Flatwound strings
  • Weight: 1,97 kg

Information:
It is a vintage instrument. The condition description corresponds to this when you come to me in the shop and would take the instrument directly with you. The temperature gradient when shipping with air freight can lead to changes in the condition of the instrument. e.g. Celluloid pickguard could tear or shrink due to tension. Likewise, pickups due to the wire expansion and shrinkage could lose their function by loosening the solder points. I am very accommodating with complaints, but please note this and do not expect a new instrument.

Please look closely at the pictures and ask as many questions as possible if something is unclear. Prefer a few more questions than to have the surprise afterwards. The shipment is securely packed. You are welcome to take a look at my website for insights, I will show you how I pack and ship by using pictures. There are also other high-resolution pictures here.

item.title: Ovation Pro Series Elite TX E-Acoustic Bass B778TX-5, Black Textured (B778TX-5-G) — $1,149.14
item.description:
$1,149.14 · Brand New · Musician Madness

Elite

Ovation Elite models are special, both for the eyes and the ears.

They dispense with the classic sound hole in the middle of the top in favour of several smaller openings at the edge of the guitar, called Multi Soundholes. This special construction has two positive effects: firstly, it increases the available vibration surface in the centre of the soundboard, and secondly, the sound comes out much closer to the ear of the person playing.

Visually, the Multi Soundholes are unique in themselves. Some models are additionally decorated with elegant epaulettes. A guaranteed eye-catcher on all stages.

Another contributor towards the appealing acoustic sound of these instruments are their high-quality spruce tops made of grade A woods.

But what would an Ovation be without the typical electro-acoustic sound via amp or PA?
This is provided by an OCP1K pickup, whose extra-large piezo crystals pick up the vibrations not only from the strings but also from the top. 
For further processing of the signal, an OP-Pro preamp is used in this model series.

The instruments of the Elite TX series are particularly unique, and it is no coincidence that they are very popular in the rock and metal scene. Those who are used to playing electric guitars especially benefit enormously from features like the maple neck, which not only stands for a fast response and good sustain, but is also equipped with a flat neck profile for optimal playability.

As an added bonus, all TX models are finished in a beautiful, genre-matching Black Textured finish.

The Elite TX Series range includes guitars of various body depths and scales. Among them are both 6- and 12-string models, as well as a 4-string bass. 

The guitars are delivered without a case. For those who want to take their instrument on tour, we recommend an Ovation 8158K-0 ABS case for Deep Contour and Mid Depth models, an Ovation 8117K-0 ABS case for Super Shallow models and for the bass, the Ovation MC-4 case.

A class of its own.

Exceptional features, outstanding sounds and a consistently high level of quality are the basic recipe for the Ovation Pro Series.

Over the last five decades, Ovation has made a name for itself through groundbreaking developments and modern designs, especially in circles of advanced and professional musicians. Legendary world stars have been swearing by the instruments of the American high-end maker for decades, and so model names such as Balladeer, Legend and Elite contribute significantly to the name recognition of the Ovation brand to this day. All these historic models, with their specific features, are combined in the Pro Series and still form the core range of Ovation Guitars.

item.title: Sandberg California TM4 Nighthawk Plus Copper — $2,800
item.description:
$2,800 · Excellent · Rocketweezie Sales

I bought this new from a shop in Spain. Customs took way too long to clear the package, so in the meantime I purchased a different bass. I can't justify keeping this. I've played it to verify functionality.

There are faint lines in the finish on the back where the body pieces come together and one moves into the bottom. These are not visible at all on the front. They are actually difficult to see on the back as well. Personally I have to tilt the bass just right in direct light to see them. I tried my best to capture them in pictures. The lighter colored pictures are super-zoomed in. I'm happy to try to take more pics if you would like.

Sandberg gig bag included

item.title: Fender USA Jazz Bass 2009 — $1,250
item.description:
$1,250 · Very Good · Haus of Gear

2009 American Fender Jazz Bass with nitro finish. Low action with incredible punch and twang. No case but will be double padded/packed.

item.title: Fender Pbass 1966 - Sunburst — $14,000
item.description:
$14,000 · Excellent · Art's Shop

The most amazing pbass you’ll ever play!! I’ve had both Mike Adams and Gleason guitars service this bass and both have said this is the perfect pbass. Plays amazing sounds amazing. The condition is wonderful. I’ve owned it for about 7 years.

item.title: Reverend Dub King 2025 - Present Transparent White and Reverend Teardrop Bass Case — $1,399
item.description:
$1,399 · Excellent · David's Gear Garage

The amazing Reverend Dub King with Reverend case made for the Dub King. I bought this and love it very much, but I never play it as I'm stuck on my P bass and this deserves a better home. Both bass and case are in pristine condition. Currently has flat wounds on it. Super deep and resonate. I keep my action particularly low on this bass as I find it just needed a touch and you get the rich sound you're looking for. Excellent for reggae, soul, electronic and hip hop stuff. Great instrument.

item.title: Gibson Grabber G3 (Natural) — $2,200
item.description:
$2,200 · Good · Haus of Gear

What we have here is an incredible face puncher of a bass. Three blade pickups power this beast and it has everything stock on it except for the saddle screws which were too high and rusted out. I had a hardware store cut the appropriate height screws for them.


Sometime in the last 40 years, someone before me etched the word “storm” into it which I actually think is hilarious and rock n roll.. but I get that you might not feel the same. As such, I’m offering the “storm discount” and listing this cheaper than normal.

No case but will be heavily padded and packed.

item.title: Charvel Pro-Mod San Dimas Bass PJ IV Mystic Blue Electric Bass Guitar w Case — $669.99
item.description:
$669.99 · Very Good · B Major Music

Sounds and plays so good! Straight neck. Fully functional. Slight cosmetic blemishes, nothing major.
